name: Moong Dal Pani
instructions: Wash and soak 1/4 cup yellow moong dal for 30 minutes. Drain.
Boil 2 cups water in a pan. Add the dal, pinch of turmeric, and salt.
Cook until the dal is very soft and mushy. Mash it well.
Adjust consistency with warm water if needed. Serve warm.

name: Curd Rice with Pomegranate
instructions: In a bowl, combine the cooled rice and yogurt.
Add lemon juice, turmeric powder, black pepper, and salt.
Mix well until the rice is coated evenly.
Gently fold in the pomegranate seeds and chopped cilantro.
Serve chilled. This recipe supports digestion and offers antioxidants.

name: Stewed Apple
instructions: Combine apples, water, honey, cinnamon, nutmeg, and lemon juice in a saucepan.
Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until apples are tender.
Serve warm. This can help soothe a sore throat or aid digestion.

name: Buttermilk (Chaas)
instructions: Combine buttermilk, cold water, green chillies, ginger, and salt in a pitcher.
Mix well.
Add ice cubes if desired.
Serve chilled.  This is a soothing drink for digestion and a healthy alternative to sugary beverages.

name: Khichdi
instructions: Wash rice and dal thoroughly until water runs clear.
Heat ghee/oil in a pot over medium heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them splutter.
Add turmeric powder, ginger powder, and asafoetida (if using). Sauté for 30 seconds.
Add washed rice and dal to the pot. Stir for 2-3 minutes.
Add water and salt. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until rice and dal are cooked and mushy.
If using, add chopped vegetables during the last 10 minutes of cooking.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves (optional). Serve hot.

name: Methi Thepla
instructions: Heat oil in a pan.
Add methi seeds and saute until fragrant (about 2-3 minutes).
Add turmeric powder, ginger powder, asafoetida, and red chili powder (if using).  Saute for 30 seconds.
Add the flour and salt to the pan.
Roast the mixture continuously, stirring constantly, for 5-7 minutes, until golden brown and crispy.
Gradually add warm water, kneading to form a soft dough. Don't add too much water.
Divide the dough into small balls.
Flatten each ball into a thin disc (about 6 inches diameter).
Serve warm as a healthy snack or light meal.

name: Karela Juice
instructions: Wash the karela thoroughly under running water.
Cut the karela into small pieces – about 1-inch cubes is ideal.
Add the karela pieces to a blender along with 5-6 cups of water.
Blend until smooth. If needed, strain the juice through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove pulp.
Add lemon juice, ginger, and black salt (if using). Stir well.
Serve immediately for maximum nutritional benefits. Can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

name: Ragi Dosa
instructions: Mix ragi flour, water, and salt in a bowl. Let it ferment for 8-12 hours.
Add chopped vegetables (if using) to the fermented batter.
Heat a non-stick tawa or skillet.
Spread a thin layer of batter evenly.
Drizzle oil or ghee around the edges.
Cook until the dosa turns golden brown and crispy (about 2-3 minutes).
Serve hot with chutney or sambar.

name: Oats Upma
instructions: Heat oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and green chili (if using). Let it splutter.
Add turmeric powder and saute for a few seconds.
Add oats and semolina.  Roast for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly, until lightly browned.
Add salt to taste.
Slowly pour hot water (about 2 cups) while stirring continuously to avoid lumps.  Add more water if needed for desired consistency.
Cook for 3-5 minutes, or until the oats and semolina are cooked and the upma is fluffy.
Garnish with chopped coriander leaves (if using) and serve hot.

name: Jamun Smoothie
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a blender.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Add more milk if needed to reach desired consistency.
Taste and adjust sweetness if desired.
Pour into a glass and enjoy immediately.

name: Garlic Vegetable Soup
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add garlic and onion; sauté for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
Add carrots and celery; cook for another 5 minutes.
Pour in vegetable broth and add potatoes.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until vegetables are tender.
Add zucchini, thyme, and pepper; simmer for 5 more minutes.
Season with salt to taste.
Garnish with fresh parsley (if desired) before serving.

name: Sprouts Salad
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a large bowl.
Gently toss to coat.
Serve immediately for maximum freshness and enzyme activity.
This salad is best consumed soon after preparation to retain nutritional benefits.

name: Oats Idli
instructions: Soak rice and oats together with fenugreek seeds, turmeric, ginger and salt in water for 4-6 hours.
Drain the soaked mixture well.
Grind the mixture to a smooth batter. Add more water if needed to achieve a slightly thick consistency.
Grease idli molds with oil.
Pour the batter into the molds and steam for 15-20 minutes or until cooked through.

name: Bajra Roti
instructions: Mix bajra flour, salt, and optional ghee/oil in a bowl.
Gradually add water, mixing until a soft, pliable dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes.
Divide the dough into small balls (about 1-2 inches).
Roll each ball into a thin roti on a greased surface.
Cook on a hot tawa or griddle until golden brown on both sides.

name: Grilled Eggplant
instructions: Preheat grill to medium heat.
In a bowl, toss eggplant slices with olive oil, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Place eggplant on the grill and cook for 5-7 minutes per side, or until tender and grill marks appear.
Garnish with fresh herbs (if using) before serving.

name: Paneer Bhurji
instructions: Crumble 200g paneer into small pieces.
Heat 1 tbsp oil. Sauté chopped onion until golden.
Add chopped tomato, green chili, turmeric, and salt. Cook for 3 minutes.
Add crumbled paneer. Mix well and cook on medium heat for 5 minutes.
Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with roti.

name: Soya Chunks Curry
instructions: Soak soya chunks in hot water for 15 minutes. Squeeze out excess water.
Heat oil, sauté onion and ginger-garlic paste until golden.
Add pureed tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and chili powder. Cook 5 minutes.
Add the soya chunks and mix well. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Garnish with coriander and serve with rice or roti.

name: Chickpea Salad
instructions: Boil 1 cup chickpeas until tender (or use canned, drained).
Dice cucumber, tomato, and onion finely.
Mix all vegetables with the chickpeas in a bowl.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, salt, and a pinch of cumin powder.
Toss well and serve chilled.

name: Moong Dal Cheela
instructions: Soak 1 cup moong dal for 4 hours. Drain and grind to a smooth batter with water.
Add chopped green chili, grated ginger, cumin, and salt to the batter.
Heat a tawa and lightly grease with oil.
Pour a ladleful of batter and spread into a thin crepe.
Cook on medium heat until golden on both sides. Serve with green chutney.

name: Tofu Stir Fry
instructions: Press firm tofu and cut into cubes.
Heat sesame oil in a pan. Add minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds.
Add tofu cubes and pan-fry until golden on all sides.
Add sliced bell peppers and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add soy sauce and a pinch of pepper. Toss well and serve hot.

name: Spinach Soup
instructions: Wash and blanch 2 cups spinach leaves. Drain and blend to a puree.
Heat butter, sauté chopped onion and garlic until soft.
Add the spinach puree and 1 cup water. Bring to a boil.
Add 2 tbsp milk, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Serve hot with a drizzle of cream if desired.

name: Poha with Peanuts
instructions: Rinse poha in water and drain. Set aside for 5 minutes.
Heat oil. Add mustard seeds and let them splutter. Add curry leaves.
Add peanuts and roast until golden. Add chopped onion and sauté.
Add turmeric, salt, and the soaked poha. Mix gently.
Cook for 3 minutes. Squeeze lemon juice and garnish with coriander.

name: Date and Nut Laddu
instructions: Remove seeds from 1 cup dates. Chop roughly.
Dry roast almonds and cashews. Let cool and chop coarsely.
Blend dates in a food processor until a sticky paste forms.
Mix the date paste with chopped nuts and a little desiccated coconut.
Roll into small balls. Coat with coconut. Refrigerate for 30 minutes before serving.

name: Beetroot Salad
instructions: Boil or steam 2 medium beetroots until tender. Peel and dice.
In a bowl, combine diced beetroot with finely chopped coriander.
Add lemon juice, cumin powder, and salt to taste.
Toss gently and let it marinate for 10 minutes.
Serve chilled as a side dish or snack.

name: Methi Paratha
instructions: Wash and finely chop 1 cup fresh methi leaves.
Mix wheat flour, methi, salt, ajwain, and 1 tsp oil. Knead into a soft dough with water.
Divide dough into balls and roll into flat rounds.
Cook on a hot tawa with a little ghee/oil until golden spots appear on both sides.
Serve hot with curd or pickle.

name: Dal Chawal
instructions: Wash rice and dal separately. Pressure cook dal with turmeric and salt until soft.
Cook rice in a separate pot or pressure cooker until fluffy.
In a small pan, heat ghee and add cumin seeds. When they splutter, pour this tempering over the cooked dal.
Serve hot dal with the cooked rice. This is a complete, easy-to-digest protein source.

name: Lauki Sabji with Roti
instructions: Peel and chop lauki into small cubes. Sauté in a pan with cumin seeds and turmeric.
Add a little water, cover and cook until soft and mushy.
Knead whole wheat flour into a soft dough and roll into rotis.
Cook rotis on a flat griddle (tawa) until done. Serve hot with the lauki sabji.

name: Ragi Roti with Mixed Veg
instructions: Mix ragi flour with warm water and a pinch of salt to make a soft dough.
Roll into flat rotis and cook on a tawa with minimal oil.
Sauté chopped beans, carrots, and peas with light spices until tender.
Serve the nutrient-dense ragi roti with the fiber-rich mixed vegetable curry.

name: Dal Palak (Spinach Dal)
instructions: Cook moong dal with turmeric until soft.
Sauté chopped spinach with garlic in a little oil until it wilts.
Mix the cooked dal and spinach together. Add salt and a squeeze of lemon juice (vitamin C helps iron absorption).
Serve warm with rice or roti.

name: Ginger Tea
instructions: Boil 1 cup of water with a 1-inch piece of crushed fresh ginger for 5 minutes.
Add tea leaves (optional) and simmer for another minute.
Strain into a cup and add honey if desired.
Sip slowly to relieve bloating, nausea, or indigestion.

name: Moong Dal Soup
instructions: Boil washed moong dal with turmeric and crushed ginger in plenty of water.
Cook until the dal is completely dissolved into the water.
Strain if you prefer a clear soup, or whisk for a thicker consistency.
Add black pepper and salt. Serve hot. Excellent for bloating and recovery.

name: Brown Rice with Dal
instructions: Soak brown rice for 1 hour before cooking (it takes longer than white rice).
Boil or pressure cook the rice until tender.
Cook dal with added vegetables like spinach or bottle gourd for extra fiber.
Serve a small portion of brown rice with a large portion of dal. The fiber helps manage blood sugar.

name: Ginkgo Biloba Tea
instructions: Boil 1 cup of water in a pan.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or a few fresh leaves.
Simmer on low heat for 5-7 minutes to extract the nutrients.
Strain the tea into a cup. Add honey for a touch of sweetness if desired.
Drink warm. It's known for supporting cognitive function and circulation.

name: Turmeric Milk
instructions: Pour 1 cup of milk into a small saucepan and heat over medium-low heat.
Whisk in 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric powder, a pinch of black pepper, and a small piece of cinnamon.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer (do not boil) for 5 minutes.
Turn off the heat. Remove the cinnamon stick.
Stir in honey or maple syrup if desired for sweetness.
Pour into a mug and drink warm before bed to soothe a cough or sore throat.

name: Hot Vegetable Soup
instructions: Finely chop all vegetables (carrots, beans, etc.) and mince the garlic and ginger.
Heat a little butter or oil in a pot and sauté garlic and ginger for a minute.
Add the vegetables and sauté for 2-3 minutes.
Add 3-4 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer until the vegetables are tender.
Add salt and black pepper to taste. Serve steaming hot.
